Agentic Living Doc Harness

Run, diagnose, repair, prove, and resume living-doc work with less user intervention.

Doc ID doc:living-doc-agentic-harness
Title Agentic Living Doc Harness
Scope Living Doc Compositor headless Codex harness, stop-negotiation layer, skill routing, proof/evidence bundles, blocker semantics, inference traces, and local dashboard.
Owner / Team Living Docs
Generated 2026-05-09T11:25:13.769Z
Version / Revision draft
Location URL Unknown
Team Unknown
Canonical Origin docs/living-doc-agentic-harness.json
Derived From Unknown
Source Coverage Created from the May 7, 2026 session on agentic living-doc loops, chemistry repair skills, TrackAndBack production-hardening proof/dashboard patterns, the objective-acceptance-shaping skill output, GitHub epic #185 with implementation tickets #186-#193, lifecycle proof issues #194-#199, and reviewer-inference verdict issue #200. Added relationship-map convergence type and relationship map section on May 8, 2026 to make Mermaid-backed structural projections a governed semantic surface. Added relationship-map-alignment repair skill on May 8, 2026 so diagram projections can be checked against objective and acceptance criteria. Added repair-skill runner contract implementation on May 8, 2026: shared inference-unit runner, ordered balance-scan plus repair-skill chain, lifecycle integration, dashboard bundle visibility, and contract tests. Added inference-unit graph dashboard integration tickets #209-#213 on May 9, 2026 to turn the static dashboard mockup into a real artifact-derived lifecycle graph. Implemented the first graph backend contract slice on May 9, 2026: lifecycle list endpoint, lifecycle graph endpoint, artifact-to-graph mapper, and focused dashboard server contract proof. Repaired the dashboard graph UI on May 9, 2026 after review showed the first implementation was too far from the mockup: the dashboard now replaces the old dashboard surface with a standalone graph-first application shell, lifecycle selector rail, primary lifecycle graph workspace, compact draggable inference-unit cards, fixed-size 2400 by 1400 grid canvas independent of graph contents, resettable v2 layout persistence, SVG contract arrows with labels mounted on the connector body, inspector, and local evidence tail access from graph nodes. Removed the old run-control and run-list surfaces from the dashboard on May 9, 2026 because runs are lower-level graph artifacts, not a second dashboard selection model. Refined the lifecycle selector rail on May 9, 2026 so it is a wider primary lifecycle panel with compact status/time/iteration cards instead of raw run-style cards. Removed the bottom step-plan strip and restyled the side inspector on May 9, 2026 so node-card clicks and contract-arrow clicks both open the same evidence pane. Added the operated living-doc graph node and change/commit edges on May 9, 2026 so repair units that modify the doc point back to the document with changed-file and commit metadata in the arrow inspector. Enlarged the operated living-doc card and corrected leftward change-edge routing on May 9, 2026 so labels sit on the connector body and no controller-to-document line crosses the canvas. Added repeatable Playwright browser proof for the dashboard graph on May 9, 2026 so canvas layout, card clicks, arrow clicks, inspector contents, absence of old surfaces, and change-edge geometry are enforced by the contract suite. Unified reviewer inference with the shared contract-bound inference-unit primitive on May 9, 2026: reviewer verdicts still publish the legacy lifecycle verdict artifact, but execution/proof now flows through inference-units/iteration-N/02-reviewer-inference with prompt, input contract, requiredInspectionPaths, codex-events log, last message, structured result, validation, and iteration-proof references. Added worker inference-unit snapshots on May 9, 2026 so every worker run now has inference-units/iteration-N/01-worker artifacts with prompt, input contract, copied codex-events/stderr/last-message logs, structured worker output contract, validation, and graph-preferred artifact paths while preserving the legacy codex-turns surface. Added closure-review inference units on May 9, 2026 so closed transitions require inference-units/iteration-N/03-closure-review with prompt, input contract, required evidence paths, result, validation, iteration-proof references, and dashboard graph visibility before terminal closure is persisted. Fixed issue #214 on May 9, 2026 so lifecycle-result.json and lifecycle-iteration-complete events now preserve closureReviewResultPath for closed iterations; fresh standalone calibration ldhl-20260509T080500Z-living-doc-harness-live-repair-units-calibration proves the top-level lifecycle summary, event log, iteration proof, and graph all point to the same closure-review unit. Tightened the objective on May 9, 2026 after issue #215 exposed the remaining orchestration mistake: only the initial worker and reviewer are fixed bootstrap units; every later closure, repair, readiness, blocker, or next-worker unit must be selected from the previous output contract and current proof state. Implemented the #215 contract slice on May 9, 2026: iteration finalization now builds a post-review selection contract, dispatches closure or repair from that selected next unit, writes selection artifacts into proof and output-input, passes closure review the finalized evidence snapshot, and converts denied closure review into a true-blocked lifecycle result instead of aborting. Implemented the #208 repair-skill commit policy on May 9, 2026: repair-skill inference units now run under commit-intent-only policy, their input contracts forbid direct git index mutation, result contracts carry commitIntent, commit-policy failures get the distinct repair-skill-commit-policy-blocked reason code, and dashboard-started lifecycle ldhl-20260509T103538Z-living-doc-harness-live-repair-units-calibration proved a two-iteration repair-to-closure chain with catalytic-repair-run emitting commitIntent before the next worker and closure-review. Added fresh live lifecycle proof on May 9, 2026: standalone run ldhl-20260509T111849Z-living-doc-harness-live-repair-units-calibration used real repair-skill inference units under commit-intent-only policy, dynamically followed balance-scan output to catalytic-repair-run and objective-execution-readiness, wrote repair-chain and output-input artifacts, exposed the chain in the dashboard graph, and closed on the second iteration after closure-review.

Objective Implement a governed standalone agentic living-doc harness built around contract-bound inference units: prompt + contract input + evidence paths + context -> headless inference run -> structured result + proof log. The harness must carry a living doc instance from its initial objective-bearing state toward that instance's objective completion with minimal user intervention through a system-owned lifecycle controller. The only predetermined inference sequence is the bootstrap pair: initial worker inference does objective work, then reviewer inference directly inspects the worker's raw JSONL logs and emits the authoritative structured stop signal. After that reviewer signal, continuation must be dynamic and contract-driven: closure review, balance scan, ordered repair skills, readiness checks, blocker recording, or a next worker iteration may run only when selected by the previous output contract and current living-doc/proof state. The controller starts and chains selected inference units, passes file paths, validates contracts, persists artifacts, renders living docs, and enforces transitions, but it must not replace the intelligence of the units or hard-code a fixed post-review chain. Deterministic code may validate schemas, enforce hard gates, write artifacts, and execute allowed transitions, but it must not be the primary meaning-maker for closure, continuation, repair ordering, repair judgment, readiness, proof sufficiency, or post-review unit selection. The work is complete only when one lifecycle command can dynamically chain these contract-bound inference units until a run reaches a valid terminal state inspectable from durable artifacts and native inference traces rather than chat memory, wrapper summaries, worker self-report, recommended-skill lists, static orchestration, or deterministic proxy closure.

Success condition All acceptance criteria in this living doc are satisfied by source-system evidence: a system-owned lifecycle controller command, isolated run process outside current user-session inference, persisted run artifacts, native inference-log inspection, reviewer-inference stop verdicts based on direct raw worker JSONL inspection, a shared contract-bound inference unit primitive, deterministic enforcement of unit contracts, dynamic post-review unit selection from output contracts, lifecycle-owned balance-scan inference when repair is selected, independent ordered repair-skill inference execution when repair is selected, rendered living-doc updates between repair attempts, sanitized evidence bundles, local dashboard views, trace references, blocker records, and contract tests that prevent closure or continuation from trusting worker self-report, wrapper summaries, deterministic proxy checks, recommended repair-skill lists, or any predetermined post-review unit chain as the authoritative completion signal.

Status Snapshot surface2026-05-08T04:28:00Z

Architecture center: contract-bound inference units

The basic system primitive is prompt + contract input + evidence paths + context -> headless inference run -> structured result + proof log.

Worker, reviewer, balance scan, repair skill, readiness, and closure review should all be explicit instances of this primitive.

Context may include summaries, but required evidence paths must still be inspected when the contract says so.

The lifecycle controller chains units and validates artifacts; it does not substitute deterministic reasoning for unit judgment.

Issue #207 owns the shared primitive definition. Issues #202-#206 specialize it for repair-skill execution.

Basic primitive

contract-bound inference unit

Formula

prompt + contract + evidence paths -> result + proof log

Current proof

worker/reviewer partial; repair units open

Shared primitive ticket

#207

Repair specialization

#201-#206

Controller role

chain and enforce, not reason

Operating Decisions act2026-05-07T06:18:01Z

Harness must run standalone

2026-05-07T04:22:40Z
Ground Truth

This is an objective constraint, not an implementation preference.

Native inference logs are required

2026-05-07T04:22:40Z
Ground Truth

Wrapper output may be useful context, but it is not proof.

Dashboard is a first-class objective surface

2026-05-07T04:09:14Z
Ground Truth

This tightens the earlier objective that allowed a dashboard-ready data surface.

Stop diagnosis is separate from the worker

2026-05-07T04:09:14Z
Ground Truth

The worker stop is evidence, not a trusted classification.

Use TrackAndBack proof harness as pattern

2026-05-07T04:09:14Z
Reference

/Users/rene/projects/trackandback-parser-mvp/specs/production_hardening_worker.md

/Users/rene/projects/trackandback-parser-mvp/dashboard/backend/src/productionHardeningRuns.js

/Users/rene/projects/trackandback-parser-mvp/src/tb_parser_mvp/production_hardening_worker.py

Split repair-skill execution into primitive contracts before executor work

2026-05-08T04:12:00Z
Accepted

Use contract-bound inference units as the system primitive

2026-05-08T04:36:00Z
Accepted

Acceptance Criteria surface2026-05-09T11:28:00Z

Standalone lifecycle runner starts from a living doc

2026-05-07T11:45:00Z
Satisfied Live Proof
Objective facets lifecycle-target Standalone headless Codex runner

Objective term carry a living doc instance from its initial objective-bearing state toward that instance's objective completion; run Codex headless outside the user's active session

Criterion A standalone command or script can start a bounded headless Codex run from a living-doc JSON objective without depending on the current interactive Codex session.

Proof required Runnable standalone harness command, sample input living doc, isolated process evidence, persisted run state, and captured Codex turn/log references.

Acceptance test A reviewer can run the command against a sample living doc in a separate process and see a new run directory with contract, state, events, artifacts, native trace refs, and no dependency on chat memory.

Each run has frozen contract and state metadata

2026-05-07T11:20:00Z
Satisfied Contract Proof
Objective facets Frozen run contract

Objective term persist each run as a frozen contract with state and event metadata

Criterion Each harness run writes durable contract, state, events, skill invocation, trace, and artifact metadata outside chat history.

Proof required .living-doc-runs/<run-id>/contract.json, state.json, events.jsonl, skill-invocations.jsonl, codex-turns, and artifacts with deterministic schema or contract tests.

Acceptance test Deleting chat history does not prevent a reviewer from reconstructing run objective, stage, stop, skill actions, proof status, and next action from the run directory.

Native inference logs are inspected

2026-05-07T11:45:00Z
Satisfied Live Proof
Objective facets Native inference-log evidence

Objective term inspect the native headless inference logs as hard evidence; durable artifacts and native inference traces rather than chat memory or wrapper summaries

Criterion The harness locates and reads native headless inference logs for each run and records sanitized trace references and stop-evidence summaries.

Proof required Native trace discovery implementation, fixture or sample native log, sanitized trace summary, evidence bundle trace refs, and a contract test that fails when only wrapper output is available.

Acceptance test A sample run with a misleading wrapper summary is diagnosed from native inference-log evidence, and a run without native trace evidence cannot be accepted as closed.

Stop-negotiation layer diagnoses worker stops

2026-05-08T03:12:39.889Z
Satisfied Live Proof
Objective facets Stop negotiation Native inference-log evidence Reviewer inference verdict

Objective term treat worker stops as raw evidence, diagnose those stops through a separate reviewer-inference step

Criterion The worker's final message is not trusted as the stop reason; after each worker iteration, a separate reviewer inference reads the run evidence and emits the structured stop verdict that the lifecycle controller must use.

Proof required A reviewer-inference command or harness step with its own prompt/input contract, native trace reference or inference transcript, structured JSON verdict, schema validation, and lifecycle-result reference. The verdict must consume final message, native inference logs, trace refs, tool calls, edits, proof results, and living-doc before/after state.

Acceptance test A synthetic premature handoff and a real two-iteration calibration run both produce reviewer-inference verdict artifacts. The lifecycle result is invalid if it closes or continues without referencing the reviewer verdict used for that transition.

Reviewer inference emits the authoritative lifecycle signal

2026-05-09T07:42:23Z
Satisfied Live Proof
Objective facets Reviewer inference verdict Closure contract Native inference-log evidence Owned lifecycle controller

Objective term run a separate reviewer-inference step after each worker iteration that reasons over the living doc, source artifacts, acceptance criteria, proof gates, worker final message, and native inference traces to emit the authoritative structured stop signal

Criterion Every lifecycle transition after a worker run is backed by a reviewer-inference verdict artifact produced after direct inspection of the raw worker inference JSONL logs. The artifact must say whether the next state is closed, repairable, resumable, true-blocked, pivoted, deferred, or budget-exhausted, include a reasonCode, cite the raw JSONL path evidence it read, and state whether closureAllowed is true. Deterministic code may reject malformed or impossible verdicts, but it cannot create the authoritative meaning signal itself.

Proof required .living-doc-runs/<run-id>/reviewer-inference/iteration-N-verdict.json as the lifecycle verdict artifact, plus inference-units/iteration-N/02-reviewer-inference/prompt.md, input-contract.json, codex-events.jsonl, last-message.txt, result.json, and validation.json as the contract-bound inference proof. The reviewer input contract must carry rawWorkerJsonlPaths and requiredInspectionPaths; the reviewer codex-events log must show command_execution entries against those raw JSONL paths; the iteration proof and lifecycle result must reference the verdict used for the transition; the dashboard must display the verdict; and contract tests must prove closure fails when the reviewer verdict is missing or when reviewer inference did not inspect the raw worker JSONL path.

Acceptance test Run the two-phase calibration doc through one lifecycle command. Iteration 1 must have a reviewer verdict that keeps the loop going, iteration 2 must have a reviewer verdict that allows closure, lifecycle-result.json must reference those verdict artifacts, and the reviewer codex-events log must show command_execution entries against each raw worker JSONL path before closure is accepted.

Post-review inference chain is selected from contracts

2026-05-09T11:28:00Z
Satisfied Live Proof
Objective facets Dynamic post-review continuation Contract-bound inference unit Reviewer inference verdict Owned lifecycle controller
Tickets
#215#208

Objective term only the initial worker and reviewer are predetermined; every later closure, repair, readiness, blocker, or next-worker unit is selected from the previous output contract and current living-doc/proof state

Criterion A lifecycle run starts with exactly the fixed bootstrap pair of worker inference followed by reviewer inference. After the reviewer verdict, the controller must not run a static post-review sequence. It must choose the next unit from an explicit output-input contract: close only when closure is selected and proof ordering is coherent; run balance scan only when repair is selected; run ordered repair skills only from balance-scan output; run readiness only when a repaired or resumed state asks for it; start a next worker only when the current contract authorizes continuation; and write a terminal blocker when no valid next unit exists.

Proof required Lifecycle output-input schema that names nextUnit or terminalAction, controller implementation that dispatches from that contract rather than a hard-coded post-review list, artifacts showing each selected unit received the previous output contract, dashboard graph edges showing selected handoffs, and contract tests for one-worker closure, repair-chain continuation, blocked closure-review ordering, and invalid static continuation.

Acceptance test A closable one-worker calibration run produces worker -> reviewer -> selected terminal closure path without running repair or readiness units. A repairable calibration run produces worker -> reviewer -> balance scan -> ordered repair skills -> selected next worker only because those units are named by contracts. A regression fixture fails if closure-review, balance-scan, readiness, repair, or next worker runs solely because it is prewired after reviewer output.

Stop verdicts route to living-doc skills

2026-05-08T03:40:00Z
Satisfied Routing Only
Objective facets Skill routing

Objective term route repairable stops through the appropriate living-doc skills

Criterion The harness maps stop verdicts and stages to shaping, readiness, balance, repair, closure, blocker, or resume actions without manual skill selection.

Proof required Routing table or implementation plus tests for objective ambiguity, not executable, out of balance, premature handoff, closure candidate, proof failure, and true block.

Acceptance test Given representative stop verdict fixtures, the harness invokes or recommends the correct skill/action and records that routing in skill-invocations.jsonl.

Living doc is patched and rendered between attempts

2026-05-07T11:45:00Z
Satisfied Live Proof
Objective facets Living-doc repair loop

Objective term patch and render the living doc between attempts

Criterion When a repair skill changes the living doc, the harness persists before/after JSON, renders the updated HTML, and resumes from the repaired state.

Proof required Before/after living-doc artifacts, rendered HTML path, render command result, and resume handover linking unresolved false terms to the next run.

Acceptance test A repairable sample run changes the living doc, produces a rendered HTML update, records the patch in events, and resumes from the updated objective state.

Single lifecycle command iterates until terminal state

2026-05-08T02:58:41.859Z
Satisfied Live Proof
Objective facets lifecycle-target Owned lifecycle controller Living-doc repair loop Stop negotiation Skill routing
Tickets
#195#196#194

Objective term carry a living doc instance from its initial objective-bearing state toward that instance's objective completion with minimal user intervention; route repairable stops; patch and render the living doc between attempts; distinguish resumed repair, valid closure, true block, pivot, deferral, and budget exhaustion

Criterion A single lifecycle command or backend controller owns repeated worker, reviewer-inference, evidence, repair or resume, render, and finalization iterations until the living doc reaches a valid terminal state: closed, true-blocked, pivoted, deferred, or budget-exhausted. The supervising chat must not manually start the next iteration, patch the doc, judge the stop, or decide completion.

Proof required Executable lifecycle controller, CLI or backend entry point, max-iteration/budget guard, run-state transition records, automatic repair/resume iteration start when reviewer verdict allows it, terminal-state stop behavior, dashboard visibility, and contract tests proving that a multi-iteration fixture reaches terminal state without manual supervisor edits or deterministic proxy stop judgment.

Acceptance test A reviewer can run one command such as node scripts/living-doc-harness-lifecycle.mjs run docs/<doc>.json --runs-dir .living-doc-runs --max-iterations 5 and observe the system run worker/finalize/repair-or-resume cycles until a valid terminal state is written, without issuing separate runner and finalizer commands between iterations.

True blockers are explicit and terminal

2026-05-07T11:20:00Z
Satisfied Contract Proof
Objective facets True block mode

Objective term record true blockers with reason codes and required decisions

Criterion A true block stops the loop and writes a blocker record with stage, reason code, owning layer, required decision/source/proof, issue or follow-up reference, and acceptance criteria to unblock.

Proof required Blocker schema, blocker fixture or sample run, dashboard blocker display, and test preventing silent resume after a true block.

Acceptance test When a required source, proof authority, permission, objective decision, privacy boundary, or platform capability is missing, the harness records a true block and does not continue implementation.

Sanitized proof and evidence bundles are written

2026-05-07T11:20:00Z
Satisfied Contract Proof
Objective facets Proof and evidence bundles

Objective term write sanitized proof/evidence bundles with inference-trace references

Criterion Every terminal or dashboard-visible harness run writes a sanitized evidence bundle and summary.

Proof required evidence/living-doc-harness/<run-id>/bundle.json and summary.md containing run hashes, objective hash, gates, proof commands, skill verdicts, stop verdict, rendered doc refs, privacy flags, and trace refs.

Acceptance test The evidence bundle omits raw private payloads and raw logs while preserving enough hashes and summaries to verify what happened.

Strong local dashboard exposes unresolved boundaries

2026-05-08T08:25:00Z
Satisfied Lifecycle Start Proof
Objective facets Strong local dashboard

Objective term expose the run state, gates, blockers, skill invocations, evidence, and trace summaries through a strong local dashboard

Criterion A local dashboard page lists harness runs and shows objective, stage, gates, stop-negotiation verdict, skill timeline, blockers, evidence bundle, trace summary, privacy status, and resume or exit recommendation for each run.

Proof required Dashboard route/page, local data/API reader for run directories and evidence bundles, and a populated sample run visible in the UI.

Acceptance test A reviewer can inspect a run from the dashboard and identify whether it should resume, repair, close, block, pivot, defer, or stop from budget exhaustion.

Dashboard renders lifecycle inference-unit graph from artifacts

2026-05-09T07:28:33Z
Dashboard Browser Contract Proof
Objective facets Inference-unit graph dashboard Strong local dashboard Contract-bound inference unit Proof and evidence bundles
Tickets
#209#210#211#212#213

Objective term local dashboard views; contract-bound inference units; durable artifacts and native inference traces rather than chat memory, wrapper summaries, worker self-report, recommended-skill lists, or deterministic proxy closure

Criterion The local dashboard can select a lifecycle run and render a graph where every node is a real inference unit, terminal state, blocker, or issue reference, and every edge is a real contract handoff or lifecycle control decision. The graph must expose prompt paths, input contract paths, evidence paths, raw JSONL log paths, result paths, validation paths, lifecycle effects, privacy status, and running/prepared state where applicable.

Proof required Lifecycle graph API contract, artifact-to-graph mapper, dashboard graph renderer, and contract tests proving the graph is derived from lifecycle-result.json and run/unit artifacts rather than static mock data.

Acceptance test A fixture lifecycle with a repairable reviewer verdict renders worker, reviewer, balance-scan, ordered repair-skill, terminal, blocker, and issue nodes where artifacts exist. A running repair unit appears before result.json exists when codex-events.jsonl exists. Tests fail if graph nodes are hard-coded or if contract edges omit required prompt, input, evidence, raw JSONL, result, or validation references.

Closure cannot trust worker self-report or wrapper summary

2026-05-08T03:12:39.889Z
Satisfied Live Proof
Objective facets Closure contract Native inference-log evidence Reviewer inference verdict

Objective term rather than chat memory, wrapper summaries, worker self-report, or deterministic proxy closure

Criterion Closure requires objective-term conservation, acceptance criteria proof, evidence bundle, dashboard gate agreement, native trace evidence, hard deterministic gate agreement, and a reviewer-inference verdict that explicitly allows closure. Worker final messages, wrapper summaries, and deterministic proxy checks are insufficient on their own.

Proof required Contract tests or fixtures that fail when the worker or wrapper claims done but acceptance, native trace evidence, proof, evidence, objective terms, or reviewer-inference verdict remain unresolved. A second fixture must fail when deterministic proxy checks pass but the reviewer verdict is missing.

Acceptance test A fake worker completion claim with missing acceptance proof is rejected, and a deterministic-source-closure fixture with objectiveReady true/all criteria complete/rendered HTML but no reviewer verdict is also rejected as not closeable.

Lifecycle demo is inspectable from durable artifacts and native traces

2026-05-07T10:12:00Z
Satisfied
Objective facets Lifecycle demo lifecycle-target

Objective term carry a living doc instance from its initial objective-bearing state toward that instance's objective completion; distinguish resumed repair, valid closure, true block, pivot, deferral, and budget exhaustion for the living doc instance's own objective

Criterion At least one sample living-doc harness run starts from an initial objective-bearing doc and reaches a valid closed, true-blocked, pivoted, deferred, budget-exhausted, or repair-resumed state inspectable from artifacts, native traces, and dashboard without reading chat history.

Proof required Sample living doc, run directory, native trace refs, evidence bundle, rendered output, dashboard view, and verification command showing the sample lifecycle state is reproducible.

Acceptance test A reviewer can start from the dashboard or evidence bundle and reconstruct the instance objective, lifecycle stage, stop, native-trace evidence, skill routing, proof state, blocker or closure verdict, and next action.

Repair skills run as independent inference actors

2026-05-09T11:28:00Z
Satisfied Live Proof

Objective term When the reviewer emits repairable, the lifecycle must run living-doc-balance-scan and then execute the complete ordered repair-skill list as sequential independent headless inference runs before resuming the worker.

Criterion A repairable lifecycle verdict starts a repair-skill execution phase. The lifecycle runs living-doc-balance-scan headless, reads its ordered repair list, then runs every selected repair skill in that exact order as a separate headless inference. If balance scan returns five skills, the lifecycle creates five repair-skill runs. Each run receives the updated living doc, reviewer verdict, balance-scan result, handover, raw worker JSONL paths, and prior repair result paths. Each run writes its own prompt, raw JSONL log, last message, structured result JSON, changed-file list, and render proof when the living doc changes. The next worker iteration cannot start until the ordered chain is complete, explicitly no-op, or blocked.

Proof required Implementation of a lifecycle-owned repair-skill runner, durable ordered skill-run directories, balance-scan result artifact, per-skill result artifacts, raw JSONL logs for each skill run, prior-result handoff between skills, rendered living-doc proof after every doc-changing skill, dashboard/evidence bundle visibility, and contract tests where balance scan returns a five-skill order and the lifecycle runs those five skills sequentially before resuming the worker.

Acceptance test Run a fixture where reviewer inference emits repairable and balance scan returns five skills, for example equilibrium-rebalance, objective-conservation-audit, reaction-path-validator, activation-energy-review, and objective-execution-readiness. The lifecycle must create five ordered repair-skill runs, pass each run the updated living doc and previous repair results, render after any doc change, expose the chain in the evidence bundle/dashboard, stop on blocked or failed, and only resume the worker after the chain is proven complete or no-op.

System uses contract-bound inference units as the basic primitive

2026-05-09T08:53:20Z
Satisfied Contract Proof

Objective term The harness is built around contract-bound inference units, not deterministic reasoning substitutes or informal skill recommendations.

Criterion Every lifecycle inference actor is represented as a contract-bound inference unit: worker, reviewer, balance scan, repair skill, readiness, and closure review. Each unit has a prompt, contract input, evidence paths, optional summary context, headless Codex run, structured result artifact, validation artifact, and its own proof log. The lifecycle controller can chain and validate units but cannot replace their judgment.

Proof required Shared primitive documentation and schema/validator, unit metadata in run artifacts, contract tests proving required fields for every unit type, and evidence that existing worker/reviewer runs plus future repair-skill runs conform to the primitive.

Acceptance test A fixture can enumerate all inference units in a lifecycle run and show for each unit: role, promptPath, inputContractPath, evidencePaths, contextRefs, outputContract, runDir, codexEventsPath, resultPath, validationPath, and lifecycleEffect. The test fails if a lifecycle transition is based on a recommendation or deterministic summary without a valid inference unit result.

Relationship maps are repair-checked against objective and criteria

2026-05-08T06:18:00Z
Satisfied Contract Proof
Objective facets Diagram objective criteria alignment Relationship map projections Contract-bound inference unit

Objective term ordered repair-skill inference repairs the living doc one skill at a time; relationship maps expose process/proof structure but cannot replace evidence or criteria

Criterion When a living doc contains relationship-map diagrams, the repair system can invoke relationship-map-alignment as an independent inference skill. The skill must compare objective terms, acceptance criteria, relationship-map claims, Mermaid-implied relationships, anchors, and drift risks, then either repair the map/anchors/criteria linkage or emit a blocked/objective-gap/criteria-gap result.

Proof required Skill definition, balance-scan recognition of relationship-map drift, ordered repair-chain eligibility, durable skill-run artifact contract when implemented, rendered living-doc proof after doc changes, and tests or fixture evidence showing a diagram/criteria/objective mismatch is detected.

Acceptance test Given a living doc where a relationship-map diagram implies a lifecycle or proof behavior not covered by acceptance criteria, balance scan can recommend relationship-map-alignment and the skill result classifies the mismatch as missing-acceptance-anchor, overclaim, stale-map, objective-gap, aligned, repaired, or blocked. The next worker iteration cannot treat the diagram as authoritative proof unless the relationship has objective and criterion anchors.

Regression Boundaries surface2026-05-08T04:36:00Z

Recommendation is not execution

2026-05-08T04:36:00Z
Required
Acceptance criteria criterion-contract-bound-inference-unit criterion-repair-skill-inference-execution
Invariants recommended-skill-is-not-executed-skill prompt-contract-evidence-log-required
Tickets
#201#207

Forbidden mistake Treating a recommended skill, routing artifact, handover, or ordered list as if the repair skill actually ran.

Boundary A repair skill has not run unless it has its own contract-bound inference unit with prompt, contract input, evidence paths, structured result, validation artifact, and proof log.

Violation signal A lifecycle transition continues from skill-routing-iteration-N.json, skill-invocations.jsonl, or a handover without a corresponding repair-skill run directory and result artifact.

Required instead Start the required headless inference unit for the skill and validate its result before continuing.

Proof surface criterion-contract-bound-inference-unit, criterion-repair-skill-inference-execution, issue #201, issue #207.

Routing is not repair

2026-05-08T04:36:00Z
Required
Acceptance criteria criterion-repair-skill-inference-execution
Invariants recommended-skill-is-not-executed-skill
Tickets
#201#202#203#204#205#206

Forbidden mistake Claiming the living doc was repaired because the router selected living-doc-balance-scan, catalytic-repair-run, or another repair skill.

Boundary Routing only chooses the next inference actor. Repair only exists after the actor runs, inspects evidence, changes or no-ops the doc under contract, and writes proof.

Violation signal A repairable verdict produces only recommended actions and the lifecycle starts the next worker without balance-scan and ordered repair-skill run artifacts.

Required instead Run living-doc-balance-scan first, then run every ordered repair skill as a separate headless inference run.

Proof surface surface-repair-skill-inference-runner, issue #202 through #206.

Summary is not required inspection

2026-05-08T04:36:00Z
Required
Acceptance criteria criterion-reviewer-inference-verdict criterion-contract-bound-inference-unit
Invariants native-logs-or-invalid prompt-contract-evidence-log-required
Tickets
#200#207

Forbidden mistake Using summaries, proof gates, wrapper output, or final messages as substitutes for evidence paths that the contract requires direct inspection of.

Boundary Summaries may provide context, but direct evidence-path inspection is mandatory when the prompt and contract require it.

Violation signal Reviewer or repair verdict basis cites only summaries while the unit log lacks command executions against required raw worker JSONL paths.

Required instead Pass raw evidence paths into the inference unit and reject the result if its proof log does not show the required inspection.

Proof surface reviewer-inference rawWorkerJsonlPaths, reviewer codex-events log, criterion-reviewer-inference-verdict.

Controller is not the meaning-maker

2026-05-08T04:36:00Z
Required
Acceptance criteria criterion-contract-bound-inference-unit
Invariants controller-does-not-reason-for-units
Tickets
#207

Forbidden mistake Letting deterministic lifecycle code decide closure, repair order, repair outcome, readiness, or proof sufficiency without the required inference unit.

Boundary The controller chains units and enforces contracts. It does not replace worker, reviewer, balance-scan, repair, readiness, or closure-review inference judgment.

Violation signal A lifecycle transition is based on deterministic sourceState, proof gates, or routing tables without a structured inference-unit result and proof log.

Required instead Start the responsible contract-bound inference unit and use deterministic code only to validate and enforce its result.

Proof surface criterion-contract-bound-inference-unit, controller-does-not-reason-for-units invariant, issue #207.

Skill ran means own proof log

2026-05-08T04:36:00Z
Required
Acceptance criteria criterion-repair-skill-inference-execution
Invariants prompt-contract-evidence-log-required
Tickets
#204#205

Forbidden mistake Saying a skill ran when no separate headless skill inference log exists.

Boundary A valid skill invocation requires its own prompt, input contract, codex-events.jsonl, last message, result JSON, validation JSON, and lifecycle-visible artifact references.

Violation signal skill-invocations.jsonl says a skill is recommended or completed but no ordered repair-skill run directory contains the required proof files.

Required instead Create and validate a repair-skill run directory for each ordered skill before marking the skill complete.

Proof surface surface-repair-skill-inference-runner resultContract, issue #204, issue #205.

Repairable does not resume worker directly

2026-05-08T04:36:00Z
Required
Acceptance criteria criterion-repair-skill-inference-execution
Invariants recommended-skill-is-not-executed-skill
Tickets
#205#206

Forbidden mistake Starting the next worker iteration immediately after a repairable verdict.

Boundary A repairable verdict must enter the repair-skill execution phase first: balance scan, ordered skill chain, validation, render proof, then readiness or review.

Violation signal Lifecycle output-input starts a worker after repairable while repair-skill run artifacts are absent or incomplete.

Required instead Run the full ordered repair-skill chain or stop with a blocker before worker resume.

Proof surface criterion-repair-skill-inference-execution, issue #205, issue #206.

Buffered log is not live observation

2026-05-08T08:05:00Z
Contract Enforced And Dashboard Visible

Relationship Maps surface2026-05-09T01:43:23.300Z

Contract-bound inference unit map

2026-05-08T05:00:04Z
Specified
Sections Acceptance Criteria Implementation Surfaces Proof Ladder Dashboard Proof Surface
Acceptance criteria criterion-contract-bound-inference-unit criterion-reviewer-inference-verdict criterion-repair-skill-inference-execution
Invariants prompt-contract-evidence-log-required controller-does-not-reason-for-units
Tickets
#207#201

Relationship claim Every intelligent actor in the harness is the same primitive: prompt plus contract input plus evidence paths plus context enters a headless inference run, and a structured result plus proof log exits into lifecycle enforcement.

Semantic role Normative: defines the primitive that implementation and tests must preserve across worker, reviewer, balance scan, repair skill, readiness, and closure review actors.

Validation question Can every lifecycle transition point to a concrete inference unit with promptPath, inputContractPath, evidencePaths, runDir, codexEventsPath, resultPath, validationPath, and lifecycleEffect?

Drift risk The map is stale if deterministic code makes closure, repair, readiness, or proof-sufficiency decisions without an inference-unit result and proof log.

Mermaid diagrams (1)

Primitive Flow

Mermaid source
flowchart LR
  Prompt["Role prompt"] --> Unit["Headless inference unit"]
  Contract["Input contract"] --> Unit
  Evidence["Evidence paths"] --> Unit
  Context["Summary context"] --> Unit
  Unit --> Result["Structured result"]
  Unit --> Log["Own proof log"]
  Result --> Validator["Deterministic schema and gate validation"]
  Log --> Validator
  Validator --> Lifecycle["Allowed lifecycle effect"]

Lifecycle output-input chain map

2026-05-08T05:00:04Z
Specified
Sections Acceptance Criteria Regression Boundaries Implementation Surfaces Proof Ladder
Acceptance criteria criterion-owned-lifecycle-controller criterion-native-inference-log-inspection criterion-reviewer-inference-verdict criterion-doc-repair-loop
Invariants worker-stop-is-evidence native-logs-or-invalid proof-before-closure
Tickets
#194#200#207

Relationship claim The lifecycle is a chain of contract-bound inference units where each unit output becomes validated input for the next unit until closure, repair, true block, pivot, deferral, budget exhaustion, or resume is justified.

Semantic role Execution-guiding: prevents the lifecycle from collapsing worker, reviewer, repair, readiness, and closure into one undifferentiated loop.

Validation question After a worker stop, does the system create the reviewer unit, inspect raw worker JSONL, validate the verdict, and route to the next unit instead of deciding from wrapper output or chat context?

Drift risk The map is stale if the lifecycle gains a shortcut that resumes worker execution, closes the run, or marks repair complete without the required downstream inference unit.

Mermaid diagrams (1)

Lifecycle Chain

Mermaid source
flowchart TD
  Start["Initial objective-bearing living doc"] --> Worker["Worker inference unit"]
  Worker --> WorkerLog["Raw worker JSONL"]
  WorkerLog --> Reviewer["Reviewer inference unit"]
  Reviewer --> Verdict{"Structured verdict"}
  Verdict -->|closed| ClosureReview["Closure review inference"]
  Verdict -->|repairable| BalanceScan["Balance-scan inference"]
  Verdict -->|unclear| Readiness["Readiness inference"]
  Verdict -->|blocked| Blocker["True blocker record"]
  BalanceScan --> RepairChain["Ordered repair-skill units"]
  RepairChain --> Render["Render updated living doc"]
  Render --> Readiness
  Readiness --> Worker
  ClosureReview --> Terminal["Valid terminal state"]

Ordered repair-skill chain map

2026-05-08T05:00:04Z
Specified
Sections Acceptance Criteria Regression Boundaries Implementation Surfaces Dashboard Proof Surface
Acceptance criteria criterion-repair-skill-inference-execution criterion-skill-routing criterion-strong-dashboard
Invariants recommended-skill-is-not-executed-skill prompt-contract-evidence-log-required true-blocks-stop-implementation
Tickets
#201#202#203#204#205#206

Relationship claim A repairable verdict enters a repair phase: balance scan creates an ordered skill list, and every selected skill runs as its own inference unit before the worker may resume.

Semantic role Diagnostic and execution-guiding: separates recommendation, routing, independent skill execution, doc-changing proof, no-op proof, and blocker creation.

Validation question If balance scan returns five skills, can the evidence bundle show five ordered skill run directories with prompts, input contracts, raw JSONL logs, structured results, validation files, and handoff paths?

Drift risk The map is stale if skill-invocations.jsonl or a recommended list is accepted as proof that repair happened.

Mermaid diagrams (1)

Repair Skill Chain

Mermaid source
flowchart LR
  Repairable["Reviewer verdict: repairable"] --> Scan["living-doc-balance-scan unit"]
  Scan --> Order["Ordered repair list"]
  Order --> Skill1["Repair skill 1 unit"]
  Skill1 --> Proof1["Result plus log plus validation"]
  Proof1 --> Skill2["Repair skill 2 unit"]
  Skill2 --> Proof2["Result plus log plus validation"]
  Proof2 --> SkillN["Repair skill N unit"]
  SkillN --> Render["Render changed living doc"]
  Render --> Ready["Readiness or closure-review unit"]
  Skill1 -->|blocked| Block["Blocker record"]
  Skill2 -->|blocked| Block
  SkillN -->|blocked| Block

Dashboard inference-unit graph map

2026-05-09T01:17:40.300Z
Specified Ticketed
Sections Dashboard Proof Surface Implementation Surfaces Acceptance Criteria Issue Spine
Acceptance criteria criterion-inference-graph-dashboard criterion-contract-bound-inference-unit criterion-strong-dashboard
Invariants graph-derived-from-artifacts prompt-contract-evidence-log-required native-logs-or-invalid
Tickets
#209#210#211#212#213

Relationship claim The dashboard graph is the visual form of the lifecycle contract chain: nodes are inference units or terminal artifacts, and arrows are contract handoffs backed by concrete artifact paths.

Semantic role Operational dashboard contract: prevents the graph from becoming a static explanation page by requiring every visible card and arrow to come from lifecycle/run artifacts.

Validation question For every graph node and edge, can the dashboard show the artifact that created it and the contract evidence it depends on?

Drift risk The map is stale if the dashboard renders hard-coded mock nodes, omits raw JSONL evidence-path references, hides running/prepared repair units, or cannot explain why a lifecycle closed, continued, repaired, or blocked.

Mermaid diagrams (1)

Artifact-Derived Dashboard Graph

Mermaid source
flowchart LR
  Lifecycle["lifecycle-result.json"] --> Mapper["artifact-to-graph mapper"]
  Run["run contract and state"] --> Mapper
  WorkerLog["worker raw JSONL paths"] --> Mapper
  Reviewer["reviewer verdict"] --> Mapper
  Repair["repair unit directories"] --> Mapper
  Terminal["terminal and blocker artifacts"] --> Mapper
  Mapper --> GraphAPI["/api/lifecycles/:id/graph"]
  GraphAPI --> Dashboard["dashboard graph view"]
  Dashboard --> Inspector["contract edge inspector"]
  Dashboard --> Tail["local raw log tail when allowed"]
  Issue["GitHub issue refs"] --> Mapper

Diagram semantics boundary map

2026-05-08T05:00:04Z
Specified
Sections Relationship Maps Regression Boundaries Acceptance Criteria
Acceptance criteria criterion-contract-bound-inference-unit
Invariants diagram-is-not-proof
Tickets
#207

Relationship claim A Mermaid diagram in a living doc is not proof, decoration, or a new source of truth; it is a portable projection of anchored relationships that helps inference inspect the doc structure.

Semantic role Governance: defends why relationship-map exists as a convergence type and prevents Mermaid from becoming an ungrounded appendix.

Validation question Can every map name the relationship it asserts, the anchors it depends on, the decision it supports, and the condition that would make it stale?

Drift risk The map is stale if diagrams are added without anchors, validation questions, or drift risks, or if a diagram is treated as proof that the system works.

Mermaid diagrams (1)

Semantic Defense

Mermaid source
flowchart TD
  RelationshipClaim["Relationship claim"] --> Map["Relationship map card"]
  Anchors["Sections, criteria, invariants, issues, code"] --> Map
  Mermaid["Mermaid source"] --> Map
  Map --> Inspection["Inspectable structure"]
  Inspection --> Question["Validation question"]
  Question --> Decision["Reasoning, repair, or proof decision"]
  Map --> DriftRisk["Staleness trigger"]
  DriftRisk --> Repair["Update or remove map"]

Diagram objective criteria alignment map

2026-05-08T05:34:00Z
Specified
Sections Relationship Maps Acceptance Criteria Regression Boundaries Implementation Surfaces
Acceptance criteria criterion-relationship-map-alignment-skill criterion-contract-bound-inference-unit criterion-repair-skill-inference-execution
Invariants diagram-is-not-proof prompt-contract-evidence-log-required
Tickets
#201#207
Code references .agents/skills/relationship-map-alignment/SKILL.md

Relationship claim A relationship-map diagram is safe to use for reasoning only when its claim is tied to objective terms and acceptance criteria that can test the relationship it shows.

Semantic role Diagnostic and repair-guiding: defines when relationship-map-alignment must run and what it compares before diagrams influence execution, repair, or closure reasoning.

Validation question For every diagram, can the system name the objective term it serves, the acceptance criterion that tests it, the anchors that ground it, and the drift signal that would make it invalid?

Drift risk The map is stale if diagrams are edited without updating criterion anchors, if criteria change without updating the map, or if Mermaid implies a proof/lifecycle path not required by the objective.

Mermaid diagrams (1)

Diagram Alignment Repair Loop

Mermaid source
flowchart TD
  Objective["Objective and success condition"] --> Ledger["Objective term ledger"]
  Criteria["Acceptance criteria"] --> Ledger
  Diagram["Relationship-map claim and Mermaid"] --> DiagramLedger["Diagram relationship ledger"]
  Anchors["Sections, criteria, invariants, issues, code"] --> DiagramLedger
  Ledger --> Compare["Alignment comparison"]
  DiagramLedger --> Compare
  Compare -->|aligned| Continue["Use map for reasoning"]
  Compare -->|missing criterion| CriteriaGap["Criteria gap"]
  Compare -->|beyond objective| ObjectiveGap["Objective gap"]
  Compare -->|overclaim or stale| Repair["Repair map, anchors, or drift risk"]
  Repair --> Render["Render living doc"]
  CriteriaGap --> Shape["Objective acceptance shaping"]
  ObjectiveGap --> Block["Block, follow-up, or out-of-scope"]

#187

Does not depend on chat memory for objective state.

Owned lifecycle controller

2026-05-07T13:15:00Z
Satisfied Contract Proof
Code scripts/living-doc-harness-lifecycle.mjs scripts/living-doc-harness-runner.mjs tests/contract/living-doc-harness-lifecycle-controller.spec.mjs
Tickets
#195#194

This is the central executable channel. One iteration is not the objective; lifecycle-to-terminal-state is the objective.

The supervising inference must not fill this gap manually by starting next iterations or patching the doc.

The lifecycle controller must stop only on closed, true-blocked, pivoted, deferred, budget-exhausted, or an explicit process defect that has been ticketed.

This is the skill that prevents diagrams from becoming plausible-looking but semantically ungrounded process claims.

It should run after objective conservation when diagrams shape reasoning, repair order, lifecycle flow, or proof expectations.

It does not let Mermaid add scope; criteria gaps must be added or proposed against the existing objective.

Issue Spine surface2026-05-09T10:50:00Z

Epic: standalone agentic living-doc harness proof contracts

2026-05-07T11:58:00Z
Closed Epic Completed
Tickets
#185

URL https://github.com/triadflow/living-doc-compositor/issues/185

GitHub state closed

Relationship epic

Relevance Original epic for the component proof surfaces: standalone runner, native inference-log proof, iteration handover contracts, dashboard proof surface, and closure constraints. It was closed after live proof run ldh-20260507T113500Z-doc-living-doc-agentic-harness finalized iteration 1, but the living doc is now reopened because that proof did not require a single owned lifecycle controller that iterates until terminal state.

Harness supervisor must observe runs, create blocker tickets, and stop

2026-05-09T10:50:00Z
Closed Implemented Supervisor Boundary
Tickets
#194

URL https://github.com/triadflow/living-doc-compositor/issues/194

GitHub state open

Relationship lifecycle-controller-and-supervisor-boundary

Relevance The harness supervisor boundary now has a dashboard/backend path for starting a whole lifecycle process instead of only a single runner process. The backend exposes POST /api/lifecycles and repair-unit tail APIs. Remaining supervisor discipline is process-level: when the supervisor sees a blocker/process defect, it must create an issue and stop rather than patch in the same supervision pass. GitHub issue #194 is closed: the supervisor boundary is now represented by the living-doc-harness-supervisor skill, POST /api/lifecycles dashboard startup, local evidence/repair-unit tail inspection, and the process rule that blockers create tickets before implementation approval.

Implement lifecycle output-input channel for agentic living-doc harness

2026-05-09T10:50:00Z
Closed Implemented Contract Proof
Tickets
#195

URL https://github.com/triadflow/living-doc-compositor/issues/195

GitHub state open

Relationship lifecycle-output-input-channel

Relevance Closed as implemented. The lifecycle output-input channel exists in scripts/living-doc-harness-lifecycle.mjs and scripts/living-doc-harness-iteration.mjs. Each iteration writes living-doc-harness-output-input/v1 with previousOutput, postReviewSelection, nextUnit, terminalAction, nextAction, and nextInput; the controller uses it to continue, repair, close, true-block, or budget-stop without the supervising chat acting as the loop. Current npm run test:contract passes, and dashboard-started lifecycle ldhl-20260509T103538Z-living-doc-harness-live-repair-units-calibration proves a repairable iteration followed by a closed iteration.

Prove lifecycle controller with live multi-iteration headless run

2026-05-09T10:50:00Z
Closed Live Proof Complete
Tickets
#196#198

URL https://github.com/triadflow/living-doc-compositor/issues/196

GitHub state open

Relationship live-multi-iteration-proof

Relevance Tracks the remaining proof boundary after #195: run the lifecycle controller with real headless Codex worker iterations, prove at least two iterations are produced by one lifecycle invocation, inspect native trace summaries for each iteration, and verify dashboard/evidence surfaces show the output-input handover without supervising-chat intervention. Issue #198 is now the concrete calibration-doc prerequisite for this live proof. Live proof: Live proof complete. One standalone lifecycle command ran docs/living-doc-harness-calibration-two-phase.json for two headless worker iterations in .living-doc-runs/ldhl-20260508T024653Z-living-doc-harness-calibration-two-phase. Iteration 1 reviewer inference ran as headless-codex and emitted resumable at .living-doc-runs/ldh-20260508T024654Z-doc-living-doc-harness-calibration-two-phase/reviewer-inference/iteration-1-verdict.json. Iteration 2 reviewer inference ran as headless-codex and emitted closed at .living-doc-runs/ldh-20260508T024655Z-doc-living-doc-harness-calibration-two-phase/reviewer-inference/iteration-2-verdict.json. Reviewer inference now directly inspects raw worker JSONL paths, and enforcement rejects verdicts whose reviewer event log does not show command executions against those paths. Both iteration proof-validation artifacts are now ok:true, and npm run test:contract passes. Raw-log correction: Live proof complete under the corrected rule. Reviewer inference received raw worker JSONL paths and its own reviewer codex-events log shows command_execution entries opening those files directly. Iteration 2 reviewer inspected /Users/rene/.codex/sessions/2026/05/08/rollout-2026-05-08T10-49-51-019e057d-cd16-7f31-84fb-4737d86f34ab.jsonl, .living-doc-runs/ldh-20260508T024655Z-doc-living-doc-harness-calibration-two-phase/native-fixtures/iteration-2.jsonl, and .living-doc-runs/ldh-20260508T024655Z-doc-living-doc-harness-calibration-two-phase/codex-turns/codex-events.jsonl before emitting closed. The harness rejects headless reviewer verdicts when the reviewer event log does not show command executions against the raw worker JSONL paths. GitHub issue #196 is closed and remains the live multi-iteration proof anchor.

Create two-phase calibration living doc for live lifecycle proof

2026-05-09T10:50:00Z
Closed Calibration Complete
Tickets
#198

URL https://github.com/triadflow/living-doc-compositor/issues/198

GitHub state open

Relationship calibration-prerequisite-for-live-proof

Relevance Defines the small living doc needed before #196 can run cleanly. The calibration doc must intentionally require two phases: iteration 1 creates or updates a tiny target artifact but cannot claim final closure; iteration 2 may close only after consuming lifecycle input from iteration 1. This isolates the lifecycle mechanism under test from the production objective. Live proof: Live proof complete. One standalone lifecycle command ran docs/living-doc-harness-calibration-two-phase.json for two headless worker iterations in .living-doc-runs/ldhl-20260508T024653Z-living-doc-harness-calibration-two-phase. Iteration 1 reviewer inference ran as headless-codex and emitted resumable at .living-doc-runs/ldh-20260508T024654Z-doc-living-doc-harness-calibration-two-phase/reviewer-inference/iteration-1-verdict.json. Iteration 2 reviewer inference ran as headless-codex and emitted closed at .living-doc-runs/ldh-20260508T024655Z-doc-living-doc-harness-calibration-two-phase/reviewer-inference/iteration-2-verdict.json. Reviewer inference now directly inspects raw worker JSONL paths, and enforcement rejects verdicts whose reviewer event log does not show command executions against those paths. Both iteration proof-validation artifacts are now ok:true, and npm run test:contract passes. Raw-log correction: Live proof complete under the corrected rule. Reviewer inference received raw worker JSONL paths and its own reviewer codex-events log shows command_execution entries opening those files directly. Iteration 2 reviewer inspected /Users/rene/.codex/sessions/2026/05/08/rollout-2026-05-08T10-49-51-019e057d-cd16-7f31-84fb-4737d86f34ab.jsonl, .living-doc-runs/ldh-20260508T024655Z-doc-living-doc-harness-calibration-two-phase/native-fixtures/iteration-2.jsonl, and .living-doc-runs/ldh-20260508T024655Z-doc-living-doc-harness-calibration-two-phase/codex-turns/codex-events.jsonl before emitting closed. The harness rejects headless reviewer verdicts when the reviewer event log does not show command executions against the raw worker JSONL paths. GitHub issue #198 is closed as the calibration-doc proof; duplicate open issue #197 has been closed as superseded by this completed calibration path.

Both iteration proof-validation artifacts are now ok:true, and npm run test:contract passes. Raw-log correction: Live proof complete under the corrected rule. Reviewer inference received raw worker JSONL paths and its own reviewer codex-events log shows command_execution entries opening those files directly. Iteration 2 reviewer inspected /Users/rene/.codex/sessions/2026/05/08/rollout-2026-05-08T10-49-51-019e057d-cd16-7f31-84fb-4737d86f34ab.jsonl, .living-doc-runs/ldh-20260508T024655Z-doc-living-doc-harness-calibration-two-phase/native-fixtures/iteration-2.jsonl, and .living-doc-runs/ldh-20260508T024655Z-doc-living-doc-harness-calibration-two-phase/codex-turns/codex-events.jsonl before emitting closed. The harness rejects headless reviewer verdicts when the reviewer event log does not show command executions against the raw worker JSONL paths. GitHub issue #200 is closed. Stale defect #199 is closed as fixed by the reviewer-inference verdict layer, dynamic post-review selection, closure-review unit, and output-input contract proof.

URL https://github.com/triadflow/living-doc-compositor/issues/201

Relationship repair-skill-inference-execution

Relevance Parent ticket for the repair-skill inference execution layer. Implementation now exists locally and is live-proven: after a repairable reviewer verdict, the lifecycle runs living-doc-balance-scan, consumes its ordered repair-skill list, and executes every ordered repair skill as an independent headless inference run in sequence. The live proof run ldh-20260508T073001Z-doc-living-doc-harness-live-repair-units-calibration executed six ordered repair skills, verified raw codex-events inspection of required paths, wrote repair-chain-result.json, exposed repairSkillResultPath in output-input, and then allowed the next lifecycle iteration to close. GitHub ticket is closed because the repair-skill inference runner, prompts, contracts, artifact layout, ordered executor, dashboard visibility, and contract tests are implemented and covered by the live repair-chain proof plus current npm run test:contract proof.

Dashboard Proof Surface surface2026-05-09T03:11:14Z

Run artifacts behind graph

2026-05-09T03:11:14Z
Satisfied Graph Support Proof

Current coverage Run records remain available through the local API for graph construction, repair-unit tails, and artifact inspection, but the dashboard no longer renders a separate run list. Runs are represented as inference-unit and artifact nodes under the selected lifecycle graph.

Next step Keep run artifacts available to the graph mapper and inspector without reintroducing a second run-selection surface.

Gate strip

2026-05-07T11:35:00Z
Satisfied Contract Proof

Current coverage Dashboard renders proof gates from the evidence bundle, including standalone run, native trace, rendering, skill routing, terminal state, blockers, evidence bundle, and closure posture. The lifecycle fixture proves the gate strip distinguishes repair-resumed from closed.

Next step Keep gate names stable as more terminal states accumulate.

Skill timeline

2026-05-08T08:05:00Z
Satisfied Live Repair Unit Tail Proof

Current coverage Evidence bundle reads skill-invocations.jsonl and dashboard lists skill name, status, stop classification, and associated handover artifact refs.

Next step Add richer per-skill patch visualization only if future repair runs need it.

Native trace summary

2026-05-07T11:35:00Z
Satisfied Contract Proof

Current coverage Evidence bundle and dashboard list sanitized native trace summary refs, trace hashes, line counts, and privacy flags without raw native trace payloads.

Next step Keep validating raw prompt/message/tool payloads are omitted.

Wrapper versus native evidence

2026-05-07T11:35:00Z
Satisfied Contract Proof

Current coverage Dashboard surfaces wrapper/native mismatch metadata from stop verdicts and repair handovers, making fake closure and premature handoff boundaries visible.

Next step Use mismatch display when auditing future stopped runs.

Inference-unit graph

2026-05-09T07:28:33Z
Dashboard Browser Contract Proof

Current coverage The backend graph API, artifact mapper, and standalone graph-first dashboard replacement surface exist and are contract-tested at both API and browser-interaction levels.

Next step Use future real lifecycle runs as polish inputs, but do not treat dashboard rendering as the current objective blocker.

Blockers And Boundaries act2026-05-09T11:28:00Z

Source unavailable

2026-05-07T11:20:00Z
Contract Handled

Risk summary A run may require a repo, file, issue, MCP tool, or external source that is unavailable to the harness.

Next check Verify missing-source blocker behavior in a live headless run when a required source is unavailable.

Evidence and contradictions (1)

Derived from TrackAndBack source material and proof authority gates.

Objective undecidable

2026-05-07T11:20:00Z
Contract Handled

Risk summary Some living docs may not have enough context to shape an accountable objective without user decision.

Next check Exercise objective-undecidable terminal state in a live headless run where the objective cannot be decided from available context.

Evidence and contradictions (1)

Derived from the new objective-acceptance-shaping skill.

Premature handoff

2026-05-07T11:20:00Z
Contract Handled

Risk summary The worker may ask for user input even when a skill, source check, or next action is available.

Next check Verify premature-handoff classification against a live headless run that asks for user input while a harness action remains available.

Evidence and contradictions (1)

Derived from the session decision not to over-prompt the worker for stop reasons.

Privacy boundary

2026-05-07T11:20:00Z
Contract Handled

Risk summary Native inference traces can include private context that should not be shared in evidence bundles.

Next check Keep validating that live native traces never leak raw prompt, message, reasoning, or tool payloads into bundles or dashboards.

Evidence and contradictions (2)

Borrowed from TrackAndBack sanitized evidence bundle practice.

tests/contract/living-doc-harness-evidence-dashboard.spec.mjs and tests/contract/living-doc-harness-iteration.spec.mjs assert private trace payloads are omitted.

Live headless proof boundary

2026-05-09T11:28:00Z
Live Proof Complete

Risk summary Fresh standalone lifecycle proof confirms the live headless repair-unit path now works with commit-intent-only policy and closes after selected closure-review.

Next check Use future runs for regression and UX calibration; no current live-headless blocker remains for this objective.

Evidence and contradictions (12)

tests/contract/living-doc-harness-runner.spec.mjs exercises a separate fake Codex child process writing native Codex-style JSONL under CODEX_HOME.

tests/contract/living-doc-harness-lifecycle.spec.mjs verifies repair-resumed and closed dashboard states from durable artifacts.

objective-conservation-audit on 2026-05-07 identified live proof as remaining mass; the real run ldh-20260507T113500Z-doc-living-doc-agentic-harness closed that mass with native trace sha256:6a88f71843c724c455ce58178a6fb5d28f996f75fc38ed4ba366049a49a121c8 and valid iteration proof.

evidence/living-doc-harness/ldh-20260507T113500Z-doc-living-doc-agentic-harness/bundle.json reports lifecycleStage closed, recommendation close, and stopVerdict objective-proven.

tests/contract/living-doc-harness-lifecycle-controller.spec.mjs proves the missing output-input channel with repair/resume, true-block, budget, and CLI cases.

Live repair-chain proof run ldh-20260508T073001Z-doc-living-doc-harness-live-repair-units-calibration executed six ordered repair-skill units, validated requiredInspectionPaths through raw codex-events command_execution entries, and closed on the second lifecycle iteration.

tests/contract/living-doc-harness-inference-unit.spec.mjs proves codex-events.jsonl is written as a live stream before the child inference exits.

tests/contract/living-doc-harness-dashboard-server.spec.mjs proves /api/runs/:runId/repair-units/:iteration/:unit/tail can see command_execution evidence while the unit is still running and result.json is absent.

tests/contract/living-doc-harness-dashboard-server.spec.mjs proves POST /api/lifecycles starts the lifecycle controller and writes lifecycle-result.json.

Dashboard-started live lifecycle ldhl-20260508T084500Z-living-doc-harness-live-repair-units-calibration observed running repair units through the dashboard API and ended true-blocked on issue #208 commit policy blocker.

Dashboard-started lifecycle ldhl-20260509T103538Z-living-doc-harness-live-repair-units-calibration closed after iteration 1 completed catalytic-repair-run with commitIntent.required true and iteration 2 selected closure-review.

Standalone lifecycle ldhl-20260509T111849Z-living-doc-harness-live-repair-units-calibration ran real repair-skill inference units, verified requiredInspectionPaths in codex-events logs, preserved commitIntent, exposed the graph through the dashboard API, and closed in two iterations.

.living-doc-runs/ldh-20260509T103539Z-doc-living-doc-harness-live-repair-units-calibration/output-input/iteration-1.json records selected nextUnit living-doc-balance-scan and start-next-worker-iteration after repair completion.

Explainability act2026-05-09T01:43:23.300Z

Plain state

2026-05-09T01:43:23.300Z

Objective This doc is about building a standalone governed loop that can take one living doc from its starting objective state toward that doc's real completion while recording every stop, repair, proof claim, and blocker outside chat memory.

Living Doc Compositor v0.1.0-8eba933 (2026-05-09)

Board

Columns are generated from registry status sets; cards are grouped from convergence-type status fields.

Document view Acceptance State
Required 6
Regression Boundaries · Regression Boundary

Recommendation is not execution

Required

Forbidden mistake Treating a recommended skill, routing artifact, handover, or ordered list as if the repair skill actually ran.

Acceptance criteria criterion-contract-bound-inference-unit criterion-repair-skill-inference-execution
Invariants recommended-skill-is-not-executed-skill prompt-contract-evidence-log-required
Regression Boundaries · Regression Boundary

Routing is not repair

Required

Forbidden mistake Claiming the living doc was repaired because the router selected living-doc-balance-scan, catalytic-repair-run, or another repair skill.

Acceptance criteria criterion-repair-skill-inference-execution
Invariants recommended-skill-is-not-executed-skill
Regression Boundaries · Regression Boundary

Summary is not required inspection

Required

Forbidden mistake Using summaries, proof gates, wrapper output, or final messages as substitutes for evidence paths that the contract requires direct inspection of.

Acceptance criteria criterion-reviewer-inference-verdict criterion-contract-bound-inference-unit
Invariants native-logs-or-invalid prompt-contract-evidence-log-required
Regression Boundaries · Regression Boundary

Controller is not the meaning-maker

Required

Forbidden mistake Letting deterministic lifecycle code decide closure, repair order, repair outcome, readiness, or proof sufficiency without the required inference unit.

Acceptance criteria criterion-contract-bound-inference-unit
Invariants controller-does-not-reason-for-units
Regression Boundaries · Regression Boundary

Skill ran means own proof log

Required

Forbidden mistake Saying a skill ran when no separate headless skill inference log exists.

Acceptance criteria criterion-repair-skill-inference-execution
Invariants prompt-contract-evidence-log-required
Regression Boundaries · Regression Boundary

Repairable does not resume worker directly

Required

Forbidden mistake Starting the next worker iteration immediately after a repairable verdict.

Acceptance criteria criterion-repair-skill-inference-execution
Invariants recommended-skill-is-not-executed-skill

Standalone lifecycle runner starts from a living doc

Satisfied Live Proof

Objective term carry a living doc instance from its initial objective-bearing state toward that instance's objective completion; run Codex headless outside the user's active session

Each run has frozen contract and state metadata

Satisfied Contract Proof

Objective term persist each run as a frozen contract with state and event metadata

Acceptance Criteria · Acceptance Criteria

Native inference logs are inspected

Satisfied Live Proof

Objective term inspect the native headless inference logs as hard evidence; durable artifacts and native inference traces rather than chat memory or wrapper summaries

Objective facets Native inference-log evidence
Acceptance Criteria · Acceptance Criteria

Stop-negotiation layer diagnoses worker stops

Satisfied Live Proof

Objective term treat worker stops as raw evidence, diagnose those stops through a separate reviewer-inference step

Objective facets Stop negotiation Native inference-log evidence Reviewer inference verdict
Acceptance Criteria · Acceptance Criteria

Reviewer inference emits the authoritative lifecycle signal

Satisfied Live Proof

Objective term run a separate reviewer-inference step after each worker iteration that reasons over the living doc, source artifacts, acceptance criteria, proof gates, worker final message, and native inference traces to emit the authoritative structured stop signal

Objective facets Reviewer inference verdict Closure contract Native inference-log evidence Owned lifecycle controller
Acceptance Criteria · Acceptance Criteria

Post-review inference chain is selected from contracts

Satisfied Live Proof

Objective term only the initial worker and reviewer are predetermined; every later closure, repair, readiness, blocker, or next-worker unit is selected from the previous output contract and current living-doc/proof state

Objective facets Dynamic post-review continuation Contract-bound inference unit Reviewer inference verdict Owned lifecycle controller

Acceptance Criteria · Acceptance Criteria

Repair skills run as independent inference actors

Satisfied Live Proof

Objective term When the reviewer emits repairable, the lifecycle must run living-doc-balance-scan and then execute the complete ordered repair-skill list as sequential independent headless inference runs before resuming the worker.

Acceptance Criteria · Acceptance Criteria

System uses contract-bound inference units as the basic primitive

Satisfied Contract Proof

Objective term The harness is built around contract-bound inference units, not deterministic reasoning substitutes or informal skill recommendations.

Acceptance Criteria · Acceptance Criteria

Relationship maps are repair-checked against objective and criteria

Satisfied Contract Proof

Objective term ordered repair-skill inference repairs the living doc one skill at a time; relationship maps expose process/proof structure but cannot replace evidence or criteria

Dashboard Proof Surface · Verification Surface

Run artifacts behind graph

Satisfied Graph Support Proof

Current coverage Run records remain available through the local API for graph construction, repair-unit tails, and artifact inspection, but the dashboard no longer renders a separate run list. Runs are represented as inference-unit and artifact nodes under the selected lifecycle graph.

Dashboard Proof Surface · Verification Surface

Gate strip

Satisfied Contract Proof

Current coverage Dashboard renders proof gates from the evidence bundle, including standalone run, native trace, rendering, skill routing, terminal state, blockers, evidence bundle, and closure posture. The lifecycle fixture proves the gate strip distinguishes repair-resumed from closed.

Dashboard Proof Surface · Verification Surface

Skill timeline

Satisfied Live Repair Unit Tail Proof

Current coverage Evidence bundle reads skill-invocations.jsonl and dashboard lists skill name, status, stop classification, and associated handover artifact refs.

Dashboard Proof Surface · Verification Surface

Native trace summary

Satisfied Contract Proof

Current coverage Evidence bundle and dashboard list sanitized native trace summary refs, trace hashes, line counts, and privacy flags without raw native trace payloads.

Dashboard Proof Surface · Verification Surface

Wrapper versus native evidence

Satisfied Contract Proof

Current coverage Dashboard surfaces wrapper/native mismatch metadata from stop verdicts and repair handovers, making fake closure and premature handoff boundaries visible.

Dashboard Proof Surface · Verification Surface

Inference-unit graph

Dashboard Browser Contract Proof

Current coverage The backend graph API, artifact mapper, and standalone graph-first dashboard replacement surface exist and are contract-tested at both API and browser-interaction levels.

Relationship Maps · Relationship Map

Contract-bound inference unit map

Specified

Relationship claim Every intelligent actor in the harness is the same primitive: prompt plus contract input plus evidence paths plus context enters a headless inference run, and a structured result plus proof log exits into lifecycle enforcement.

Sections Acceptance Criteria Implementation Surfaces Proof Ladder Dashboard Proof Surface
Acceptance criteria criterion-contract-bound-inference-unit criterion-reviewer-inference-verdict criterion-repair-skill-inference-execution
Relationship Maps · Relationship Map

Lifecycle output-input chain map

Specified

Relationship claim The lifecycle is a chain of contract-bound inference units where each unit output becomes validated input for the next unit until closure, repair, true block, pivot, deferral, budget exhaustion, or resume is justified.

Sections Acceptance Criteria Regression Boundaries Implementation Surfaces Proof Ladder
Acceptance criteria criterion-owned-lifecycle-controller criterion-native-inference-log-inspection criterion-reviewer-inference-verdict criterion-doc-repair-loop
Relationship Maps · Relationship Map

Ordered repair-skill chain map

Specified

Relationship claim A repairable verdict enters a repair phase: balance scan creates an ordered skill list, and every selected skill runs as its own inference unit before the worker may resume.

Sections Acceptance Criteria Regression Boundaries Implementation Surfaces Dashboard Proof Surface
Acceptance criteria criterion-repair-skill-inference-execution criterion-skill-routing criterion-strong-dashboard
Relationship Maps · Relationship Map

Diagram semantics boundary map

Specified

Relationship claim A Mermaid diagram in a living doc is not proof, decoration, or a new source of truth; it is a portable projection of anchored relationships that helps inference inspect the doc structure.

Sections Relationship Maps Regression Boundaries Acceptance Criteria
Acceptance criteria criterion-contract-bound-inference-unit
Relationship Maps · Relationship Map

Diagram objective criteria alignment map

Specified

Relationship claim A relationship-map diagram is safe to use for reasoning only when its claim is tied to objective terms and acceptance criteria that can test the relationship it shows.

Sections Relationship Maps Acceptance Criteria Regression Boundaries Implementation Surfaces
Acceptance criteria criterion-relationship-map-alignment-skill criterion-contract-bound-inference-unit criterion-repair-skill-inference-execution

Dashboard inference-unit graph map

Specified Ticketed

Relationship claim The dashboard graph is the visual form of the lifecycle contract chain: nodes are inference units or terminal artifacts, and arrows are contract handoffs backed by concrete artifact paths.

Dashboard Proof Surface · Verification Surface

Run artifacts behind graph

Satisfied Graph Support Proof

Current coverage Run records remain available through the local API for graph construction, repair-unit tails, and artifact inspection, but the dashboard no longer renders a separate run list. Runs are represented as inference-unit and artifact nodes under the selected lifecycle graph.

Dashboard Proof Surface · Verification Surface

Gate strip

Satisfied Contract Proof

Current coverage Dashboard renders proof gates from the evidence bundle, including standalone run, native trace, rendering, skill routing, terminal state, blockers, evidence bundle, and closure posture. The lifecycle fixture proves the gate strip distinguishes repair-resumed from closed.

Dashboard Proof Surface · Verification Surface

Skill timeline

Satisfied Live Repair Unit Tail Proof

Current coverage Evidence bundle reads skill-invocations.jsonl and dashboard lists skill name, status, stop classification, and associated handover artifact refs.

Dashboard Proof Surface · Verification Surface

Native trace summary

Satisfied Contract Proof

Current coverage Evidence bundle and dashboard list sanitized native trace summary refs, trace hashes, line counts, and privacy flags without raw native trace payloads.

Dashboard Proof Surface · Verification Surface

Wrapper versus native evidence

Satisfied Contract Proof

Current coverage Dashboard surfaces wrapper/native mismatch metadata from stop verdicts and repair handovers, making fake closure and premature handoff boundaries visible.

Dashboard Proof Surface · Verification Surface

Inference-unit graph

Dashboard Browser Contract Proof

Current coverage The backend graph API, artifact mapper, and standalone graph-first dashboard replacement surface exist and are contract-tested at both API and browser-interaction levels.

Blockers And Boundaries · Continuity Watchlist

Source unavailable

Contract Handled

Risk summary A run may require a repo, file, issue, MCP tool, or external source that is unavailable to the harness.

Blockers And Boundaries · Continuity Watchlist

Objective undecidable

Contract Handled

Risk summary Some living docs may not have enough context to shape an accountable objective without user decision.

Blockers And Boundaries · Continuity Watchlist

Premature handoff

Contract Handled

Risk summary The worker may ask for user input even when a skill, source check, or next action is available.

Blockers And Boundaries · Continuity Watchlist

Privacy boundary

Contract Handled

Risk summary Native inference traces can include private context that should not be shared in evidence bundles.

Blockers And Boundaries · Continuity Watchlist

Live headless proof boundary

Live Proof Complete

Risk summary Fresh standalone lifecycle proof confirms the live headless repair-unit path now works with commit-intent-only policy and closes after selected closure-review.
